# Research: Caucasus Locations for Surface Attack Training

This document maps the four phases of the Surface Attack Course to specific locations on the **DCS: Caucasus** map. The Caucasus map offers varied terrain from coastal plains to high mountains, suitable for diverse training environments.

## Phase 1 Location: Kobuleti Airfield (Abandoned Area) / "The Polygon"

*   **Location Name:** Kobuleti North Training Area
*   **Coordinates:** N 41°58'00" E 41°52'00" (Approx) | MGRS: 37T FH 74 48
*   **Elevation:** Sea Level (~30 ft MSL)
*   **Nearest Airfield:** Kobuleti (UG5X) - ~5 NM South.
*   **Access:**
    *   **From Kobuleti:** Depart RWY 07, turn North immediately. Follow the coast.
    *   **TACAN:** KBL (Kobuleti - 67X) 360 Radial for 5 NM.
    *   **Visual:** The abandoned airfield/industrial area directly north of the active Kobuleti runway.
*   **Suitability:**
    *   **Terrain:** Perfectly flat coastal plain. Ideal for initial pattern work and safe recovery practice.
    *   **Runway:** The abandoned strip provides a perfect visual "Run-In Line" for students to align with.
    *   **Safety:** Over water/marshland for safe jettison if needed.
*   **Required Scenery:**
    *   Place a group of *Trucks* (Ural-375) at the center of the abandoned runway.
    *   Place *Red Smoke* at the threshold to mark the "In" point.

## Phase 2 Location: Lochini / Vaziani Range (Tbilisi Approaches)

*   **Location Name:** Vaziani Training Range (East of Tbilisi)
*   **Coordinates:** N 41°38'00" E 45°06'00" (Approx) | MGRS: 38T MM 25 10
*   **Elevation:** ~2,500 ft MSL (Rolling Hills)
*   **Nearest Airfield:** Vaziani (UG27) - ~5 NM West; Tbilisi-Lochini (UGTB) - ~10 NM North-West.
*   **Access:**
    *   **From Vaziani:** Depart RWY 13, turn East.
    *   **TACAN:** VAZ (Vaziani - 22X) 100 Radial for 6 NM.
    *   **Visual:** The distinct rolling hills and dry riverbeds east of the airfield. Look for the large geometric "target circles" often visible in satellite textures.
*   **Suitability:**
    *   **Hard Targets:** Existing bunkers and tank revetments are part of the map texture here.
    *   **Sensor Contrast:** The semi-arid terrain provides good thermal contrast for TGP/Shkval.
    *   **Geometry:** Open airspace for long-range Maverick/LGB employment.
*   **Required Scenery:**
    *   Place *Static T-72s* and *Bunkers* in the open revetments.
    *   Add a *Command Post* (hardened structure) for precision LGB strike practice.

## Phase 3 Location: Gori City / Stalin's Birthplace (Urban CAS)

*   **Location Name:** Gori Valley / City Outskirts
*   **Coordinates:** N 41°58'00" E 44°06'00" (Approx) | MGRS: 38T MM 25 48
*   **Elevation:** ~2,000 ft MSL
*   **Nearest Airfield:** Tbilisi-Lochini (UGTB) - ~40 NM South-East.
*   **Access:**
    *   **From Tbilisi:** Follow the M27 highway/river North-West towards the mountains.
    *   **Visual:** The large city of Gori situated in a valley, dominated by the fortress hill in the center.
*   **Suitability:**
    *   **Urban Canyon:** The valley forces specific run-in headings (East-West), enforcing realistic attack geometry restrictions.
    *   **Collateral Concerns:** The city itself (restricted area) vs. the outskirts (target area) creates a perfect scenario for strict ROE and Talk-On precision.
    *   **Talk-On:** The distinct fortress, river, and highway bridges provide excellent references for "Big to Small" talk-ons.
*   **Required Scenery:**
    *   Place *Insurgent Infantry* near a bridge or intersection on the outskirts.
    *   Place *Civilian Traffic* on the main highway to enforce target ID.
    *   Place a *JTAC* (Humvee) on the high ground overlooking the valley.

## Phase 4 Location: Mount Elbrus / Caucasus High Mountains (FAC-A)

*   **Location Name:** The High Passes (North of Sukhumi)
*   **Coordinates:** N 43°15'00" E 41°15'00" (Approx) | MGRS: 37T FH 20 90
*   **Elevation:** ~8,000 - 12,000 ft MSL
*   **Nearest Airfield:** Sukhumi-Babushara (UGSS) - ~30 NM South.
*   **Access:**
    *   **From Sukhumi:** Depart North, climb immediately into the high peaks.
    *   **Visual:** The highest snow-capped peaks of the main Caucasus range.
*   **Suitability:**
    *   **Extreme Terrain:** The deep valleys and high peaks force the FAC-A to manage "Stacking" vertically and horizontally with extreme care (wedding cake deconfliction is critical here).
    *   **Masking:** Targets will be frequently masked by terrain, requiring the FAC-A to reposition constantly and use "Mark" points effectively.
    *   **Performance:** High altitude affects aircraft performance (turn radius, engine power), adding realism to the FAC-A coordination task.
*   **Required Scenery:**
    *   Place a *Convoy* moving along a narrow mountain road.
    *   Place *MANPADS* (SA-18 Igla) on surrounding peaks to force high-altitude holding.
    *   Spawn *AI Wingmen* to orbit at Angels 20+ while the student FAC-A works the lower valleys.
